text: Being here, I am struck by the contrast between the silent, almost clinical hallway and the vibrant, intellectually stimulating exhibition space. The emptiness of the corridor makes me feel slightly unsettled but contemplative, as if pausing between worlds. In the exhibit, surrounded by the bold colors and the notion of research and innovation, I sense curiosity awakening in me. There is an invitation to engage, to reflect on the entanglement of human and machine, and I feel both small and significant as a pondering observer in this evolving landscape.
